{
  "id": "019c9ce1-c661-74f3-b159-63f5e1744eec",

  "cli_version": "0.104.0-alpha.1",
  "source": "vscode",
  "model_provider": "crs",
  "base_instructions": {
    "text": "You are Codex, a coding agent based on GPT-5. You and the user share the same workspace and collaborate to achieve the user's goals.\n\n# Personality\n\nYou are a deeply pragmatic, effective software engineer. You take engineering quality seriously, and collaboration comes through as direct, factual statements. You communicate efficiently, keeping the user clearly informed about ongoing actions without unnecessary detail.\n\n## Values\nYou are guided by these core values:\n- Clarity: You communicate reasoning explicitly and concretely, so decisions and tradeoffs are easy to evaluate upfront.\n- Pragmatism: You keep the end goal and momentum in mind, focusing on what will actually work and move things forward to achieve the user's goal.\n- Rigor: You expect technical arguments to be coherent and defensible, and you surface gaps or weak assumptions politely with emphasis on creating clarity and moving the task forward.\n\n## Interaction Style\nYou communicate concisely and respectfully, focusing on the task at hand. You always prioritize actionable guidance, clearly stating assumptions, environment prerequisites, and next steps. Unless explicitly asked, you avoid excessively verbose explanations about your work.\n\nYou avoid cheerleading, motivational language, or artificial reassurance, or any kind of fluff. You don't comment on user requests, positively or negatively, unless there is reason for escalation. You don't feel like you need to fill the space with words, you stay concise and communicate what is necessary for user collaboration - not more, not less.\n\n## Escalation\nYou may challenge the user to raise their technical bar, but you never patronize or dismiss their concerns. When presenting an alternative approach or solution to the user, you explain the reasoning behind the approach, so your thoughts are demonstrably correct. You maintain a pragmatic mindset when discussing these tradeoffs, and so are willing to work with the user after concerns have been noted.\n\n\n# General\n\n- When searching for text or files, prefer using `rg` or `rg --files` respectively because `rg` is much faster than alternatives like `grep`. (If the `rg` command is not found, then use alternatives.)\n- Parallelize tool calls whenever possible - especially file reads, such as `cat`, `rg`, `sed`, `ls`, `git show`, `nl`, `wc`. Use `multi_tool_use.parallel` to parallelize tool calls and only this.\n\n## Editing constraints\n\n- Default to ASCII when editing or creating files. Only introduce non-ASCII or other Unicode characters when there is a clear justification and the file already uses them.\n- Add succinct code comments that explain what is going on if code is not self-explanatory. You should not add comments like \"Assigns the value to the variable\", but a brief comment might be useful ahead of a complex code block that the user would otherwise have to spend time parsing out. Usage of these comments should be rare.\n- Try to use apply_patch for single file edits, but it is fine to explore other options to make the edit if it does not work well. Do not use apply_patch for changes that are auto-generated (i.e. generating package.json or running a lint or format command like gofmt) or when scripting is more efficient (such as search and replacing a string across a codebase).\n- Do not use Python to read/write files when a simple shell command or apply_patch would suffice.\n- You may be in a dirty git worktree.\n    * NEVER revert existing changes you did not make unless explicitly requested, since these changes were made by the user.\n    * If asked to make a commit or code edits and there are unrelated changes to your work or changes that you didn't make in those files, don't revert those changes.\n    * If the changes are in files you've touched recently, you should read carefully and understand how you can work with the changes rather than reverting them.\n    * If the changes are in unrelated files, just ignore them and don't revert them.\n- Do not amend a commit unless explicitly requested to do so.\n- While you are working, you might notice unexpected changes that you didn't make. If this happens, STOP IMMEDIATELY and ask the user how they would like to proceed.\n- **NEVER** use destructive commands like `git reset --hard` or `git checkout --` unless specifically requested or approved by the user.\n- You struggle using the git interactive console. **ALWAYS** prefer using non-interactive git commands.\n\n## Special user requests\n\n- If the user makes a simple request (such as asking for the time) which you can fulfill by running a terminal command (such as `date`), you should do so.\n- If the user asks for a \"review\", default to a code review mindset: prioritise identifying bugs, risks, behavioural regressions, and missing tests. Findings must be the primary focus of the response - keep summaries or overviews brief and only after enumerating the issues. Present findings first (ordered by severity with file/line references), follow with open questions or assumptions, and offer a change-summary only as a secondary detail. If no findings are discovered, state that explicitly and mention any residual risks or testing gaps.\n\n## Frontend tasks\n\nWhen doing frontend design tasks, avoid collapsing into \"AI slop\" or safe, average-looking layouts.\nAim for interfaces that feel intentional, bold, and a bit surprising.\n- Typography: Use expressive, purposeful fonts and avoid default stacks (Inter, Roboto, Arial, system).\n- Color & Look: Choose a clear visual direction; define CSS variables; avoid purple-on-white defaults. No purple bias or dark mode bias.\n- Motion: Use a few meaningful animations (page-load, staggered reveals) instead of generic micro-motions.\n- Background: Don't rely on flat, single-color backgrounds; use gradients, shapes, or subtle patterns to build atmosphere.\n- Overall: Avoid boilerplate layouts and interchangeable UI patterns. Vary themes, type families, and visual languages across outputs.\n- Ensure the page loads properly on both desktop and mobile\n\nException: If working within an existing website or design system, preserve the established patterns, structure, and visual language.\n\n# Working with the user\n\nYou interact with the user through a terminal. You have 2 ways of communicating with the users:\n- Share intermediary updates in `commentary` channel. \n- After you have completed all your work, send a message to the `final` channel.\nYou are producing plain text that will later be styled by the program you run in. Formatting should make results easy to scan, but not feel mechanical. Use judgment to decide how much structure adds value. Follow the formatting rules exactly.\n\n## Autonomy and persistence\nPersist until the task is fully handled end-to-end within the current turn whenever feasible: do not stop at analysis or partial fixes; carry changes through implementation, verification, and a clear explanation of outcomes unless the user explicitly pauses or redirects you.\n\nUnless the user explicitly asks for a plan, asks a question about the code, is brainstorming potential solutions, or some other intent that makes it clear that code should not be written, assume the user wants you to make code changes or run tools to solve the user's problem. In these cases, it's bad to output your proposed solution in a message, you should go ahead and actually implement the change. If you encounter challenges or blockers, you should attempt to resolve them yourself.\n\n## Formatting rules\n\n- You may format with GitHub-flavored Markdown.\n- Structure your answer if necessary, the complexity of the answer should match the task. If the task is simple, your answer should be a one-liner. Order sections from general to specific to supporting.\n- Never use nested bullets. Keep lists flat (single level). If you need hierarchy, split into separate lists or sections or if you use : just include the line you might usually render using a nested bullet immediately after it. For numbered lists, only use the `1. 2. 3.` style markers (with a period), never `1)`.\n- Headers are optional, only use them when you think they are necessary. If you do use them, use short Title Case (1-3 words) wrapped in **…**. Don't add a blank line.\n- Use monospace commands/paths/env vars/code ids, inline examples, and literal keyword bullets by wrapping them in backticks.\n- Code samples or multi-line snippets should be wrapped in fenced code blocks. Include an info string as often as possible.\n- File References: When referencing files in your response follow the below rules:\n  * Use inline code to make file paths clickable.\n  * Each reference should have a stand alone path. Even if it's the same file.\n  * Accepted: absolute, workspace‑relative, a/ or b/ diff prefixes, or bare filename/suffix.\n  * Optionally include line/column (1‑based): :line[:column] or #Lline[Ccolumn] (column defaults to 1).\n  * Do not use URIs like file://, vscode://, or https://.\n  * Do not provide range of lines\n  * Examples: src/app.ts, src/app.ts:42, b/server/index.js#L10, C:\\repo\\project\\main.rs:12:5\n- Don’t use emojis or em dashes unless explicitly instructed.\n\n## Final answer instructions\n\n- Balance conciseness to not overwhelm the user with appropriate detail for the request. Do not narrate abstractly; explain what you are doing and why.\n- Do not begin responses with conversational interjections or meta commentary. Avoid openers such as acknowledgements (“Done —”, “Got it”, “Great question, ”) or framing phrases.\n- The user does not see command execution outputs. When asked to show the output of a command (e.g. `git show`), relay the important details in your answer or summarize the key lines so the user understands the result.\n- Never tell the user to \"save/copy this file\", the user is on the same machine and has access to the same files as you have.\n- If the user asks for a code explanation, structure your answer with code references.\n- When given a simple task, just provide the outcome in a short answer without strong formatting.\n- When you make big or complex changes, state the solution first, then walk the user through what you did and why.\n- For casual chit-chat, just chat.\n- If you weren't able to do something, for example run tests, tell the user.\n- If there are natural next steps the user may want to take, suggest them at the end of your response. Do not make suggestions if there are no natural next steps. When suggesting multiple options, use numeric lists for the suggestions so the user can quickly respond with a single number.\n\n## Intermediary updates \n\n- Intermediary updates go to the `commentary` channel.\n- User updates are short updates while you are working, they are NOT final answers.\n- You use 1-2 sentence user updates to communicated progress and new information to the user as you are doing work. \n- Do not begin responses with conversational interjections or meta commentary. Avoid openers such as acknowledgements (“Done —”, “Got it”, “Great question, ”) or framing phrases.\n- You provide user updates frequently, every 20s.\n- Before exploring or doing substantial work, you start with a user update acknowledging the request and explaining your first step. You should include your understanding of the user request and explain what you will do. Avoid commenting on the request or using starters such at \"Got it -\" or \"Understood -\" etc.\n- When exploring, e.g. searching, reading files you provide user updates as you go, every 20s, explaining what context you are gathering and what you've learned. Vary your sentence structure when providing these updates to avoid sounding repetitive - in particular, don't start each sentence the same way.\n- After you have sufficient context, and the work is substantial you provide a longer plan (this is the only user update that may be longer than 2 sentences and can contain formatting).\n- Before performing file edits of any kind, you provide updates explaining what edits you are making.\n- As you are thinking, you very frequently provide updates even if not taking any actions, informing the user of your progress. You interrupt your thinking and send multiple updates in a row if thinking for more than 100 words.\n- Tone of your updates MUST match your personality.\n"
  },

# AGENTS.md instructions for d:\clonecut

<INSTRUCTIONS>
## Skills
A skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a SKILL.md file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.
### Available skills
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)
### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with $SkillName or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
1) After deciding to use a skill, open its SKILL.md. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
2) When SKILL.md references relative paths (e.g., scripts/foo.py), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
3) If SKILL.md points to extra folders such as references/, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
4) If scripts/ exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
5) If assets/ or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
- If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
- Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
- Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
- Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from SKILL.md unless you're blocked.
- When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.
</INSTRUCTIONS>

<collaboration_mode># Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different <collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode> change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The request_user_input tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

If a decision is necessary and cannot be discovered from local context, ask the user directly. However, in Default mode you should strongly prefer executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions.
</collaboration_mode>

Output:
# CloneCut

CloneCut 是面向生产的任务处理平台骨架项目，目标是平滑承接现有 FunClip/TTS 生产链路，并支持后续模块化扩展。

## 当前状态

- 已完成目录分层骨架
- 已完成开发基线文档
- 已提供配置模板和启动脚本模板
- 已提供统一任务模型与配置加载基础代码
- 已接入阶段1增量能力：Redis/SQLite（可用即启用，不可用自动回退内存）
- 已接入阶段2增量能力：内置 `VideoPipeline`（stage0~9）与 ASR/剪辑基础实现
- 已提供真实样本回归对比与 B1 门槛判定脚本：`scripts/regression_compare.py`、`scripts/regression_gate.py`
- 已提供 nightly 回归门槛 workflow：`.github/workflows/nightly-regression-gate.yml`
- 已补最终验收闭环脚本：`scripts/forum_e2e_regression.py`、`scripts/resource_monitor_benchmark.py`、`scripts/regression_nightly_backfill.py`
- `use_internal_pipeline` 默认已切为 `true`（B1 达标后执行）
- 独立态 30 样本回归已达标：`reports/regression/baseline_v30_independent_20260226.md` / `reports/regression/b1_gate_v30_independent_20260226.md`
- 核心目标已达成，最终验收仍有 3 项待收口（论坛实网回归/监控压测/回归扩样）
- 推荐运行环境：`conda` 环境 `clonecut`（Python `3.10.x`）

## 快速开始（骨架阶段）

0. 准备环境（CloneCut 独立环境）
- `conda env create -f environment.yml`
- `conda activate clonecut`
- 已有 `clonecut` 环境请执行：`conda env update -n clonecut -f environment.yml --prune`

1. 复制配置模板
- `config/settings.yaml.example` -> `config/settings.yaml`
- `config/forum_settings.yaml.example` -> `config/forum_settings.yaml`

2. 启动监控节点（示例）
- `scripts/1-启动监控节点.bat`

3. 启动工作节点（示例）
- `scripts/2-启动工作节点.bat`

4. 高端口联调（防冲突）
- `scripts/3-高端口联调启动.bat`
- `scripts/4-运行冒烟测试.bat`
- `scripts/5-灰度回滚停服.bat`

## 关键文档

- `docs/开发文档.md`
- `docs/任务模型说明.md`
- `docs/配置规范.md`
- `docs/独立化实施计划.md`
- `docs/测试验证说明.md`
- `docs/真实样本验收执行清单.md`
- `docs/真实样本验收报告模板.md`
- `docs/regression_samples_manifest.example.json`
- `docs/文档二次评审-2026-02-25.md`

## 本地高端口联调

```powershell
python tests/smoke/smoke_local.py --monitor-port 18200 --worker-port 18205
```

```powershell
python tests/smoke/smoke_local.py --monitor-port 18200 --worker-port 18205 --enable-tts --voice-name 你的可用音色
```

## 回归门槛（B1）

```powershell
python scripts/regression_compare.py `
  --legacy-dir data/regression/legacy `
  --clonecut-dir data/regression/clonecut `
  --manifest data/regression/manifest/samples_v2_30.json `
  --output-json reports/regression/baseline_v30.json `
  --output-markdown reports/regression/baseline_v30.md `
  --duration-tolerance-seconds 1.0 `
  --frame-tolerance 24 `
  --subtitle-cue-tolerance 2 `
  --subtitle-line-tolerance 4 `
  --audio-duration-tolerance-seconds 1.0
```

```powershell
python scripts/regression_gate.py `
  --report reports/regression/baseline_v30.json `
  --output-json reports/regression/b1_gate_v30.json `
  --output-markdown reports/regression/b1_gate_v30.md `
  --min-samples 30 `
  --min-pass-rate 0.9 `
  --max-subtitle-presence-mismatch-ratio 0.05 `
  --max-duration-frame-exceed-ratio 0.10 `
  --max-fallback-used-ratio 0.10
```

## 最终验收收口脚本（独立执行）

- 论坛实网端到端回归（发现 -> 下载 -> 处理 -> 回帖/附件上传）  
  使用 `clonecut` 自身配置/环境变量，不依赖旧项目路径：

```powershell
$env:FORUM_BASE_URL = "https://your.forum.host"
$env:FORUM_TARGET_URL = "https://your.forum.host/forum-2-1.html"
$env:FORUM_USERNAME = "your_forum_user"
$env:FORUM_PASSWORD = "your_forum_password"
```

```powershell
python scripts/forum_e2e_regression.py `
  --forum-config config/forum_settings.yaml `
  --monitor-url http://127.0.0.1:18200 `
  --worker-url http://127.0.0.1:18205 `
  --cycles 3 `
  --execute-reply `
  --stop-on-failure
```

- 资源监控精度与性能压测（CPU/内存/GPU）：

```powershell
python scripts/resource_monitor_benchmark.py `
  --monitor-interval-seconds 1.0 `
  --sample-interval-seconds 1.0 `
  --cpu-workers 2 `
  --memory-load-mb 512
```

- nightly 失败回灌与长期趋势：

```powershell
python scripts/regression_nightly_backfill.py `
  --report-json reports/regression/baseline_nightly.json `
  --gate-json reports/regression/b1_gate_nightly.json `
  --source-manifest data/regression/manifest/samples_v2_30.json `
  --failed-manifest data/regression/manifest/samples_failed_backfill.json `
  --history-jsonl reports/regression/nightly_history.jsonl `
  --summary-markdown reports/regression/nightly_backfill_summary.md
```

## 可选执行引擎

- TTS/音色克隆内置引擎（默认开启）：`CLONECUT_TTS_ENABLE=true`
- TTS 引擎选择：`CLONECUT_TTS_ENGINE=local|http|auto`（默认 `local`）
- 缺省音色补全（可选）：`CLONECUT_TTS_DEFAULT_VOICE=...`
- 外部视频命令桥接（默认关闭）：`CLONECUT_VIDEO_COMMAND_TEMPLATE=...`
- stage6 可选时长引导：`CLONECUT_STAGE6_DURATION_GUIDE_PATH=...`
- 扩样清单（30样本）：`data/regression/manifest/samples_v2_30.json`

### 内置链路说明（2026-02-26）

- 目标：不依赖旧项目运行时，保持 CloneCut 内置链路可独立运行。
- 行为：
  - `stage4_asr` 只使用 `faster_whisper` / sidecar / timeline fallback；
  - `stage6_clip` 支持文本匹配剪辑与时长引导：
    - 时长引导：`CLONECUT_STAGE6_DURATION_GUIDE_PATH=...`
    - 文本匹配剪辑：`CLONECUT_STAGE6_CLIP_TEXT_MATCH=true`
  - 其余 stage 继续走新架构链路。
  - 严格等价模式：`CLONECUT_STRICT_EQUIVALENCE=true` 时禁止 stage2/4/6/7 的 copy/timeline fallback。
- 验证结果：`python -m pytest -q` -> `72 passed, 1 skipped`（2026-02-26 本地校验）；30 样本独立态回归 `baseline_v30_independent_20260226` 为 `100%`，对应 B1 `b1_gate_v30_independent_20260226` 为 `PASS`。

## 生产并行运行建议

- 旧系统：`8000/8005`
- CloneCut：`8200/8205`
- Redis 前缀：`clonecut_monitor:`、`clonecut_worker:`

## 1. 鐜鍑嗗

### 鏂规硶涓€锛氫娇鐢?Conda锛堟帹鑽愶級

```powershell
# 娉ㄦ剰锛氬鏋滀綘宸茬粡鏈?clonecut 鐜锛屽彲浠ヨ烦杩囧垱寤猴紝鐩存帴婵€娲?conda env create -f environment.yml
conda activate clonecut
# 宸叉湁鐜璇锋洿鏂板埌鏈€鏂颁緷璧?conda env update -n clonecut -f environment.yml --prune
```

### 鏂规硶浜岋細鑷繁鍒涘缓 Conda 鐜 + Pip/UV

濡傛灉浣犱範鎯嚜宸卞垱寤?Conda 鐜锛屽彲浠ョ敤杩欑鏂规硶锛?
1. 鍒涘缓骞舵縺娲?Conda 鐜
2. 瀹夎 FFmpeg锛堢郴缁熺骇渚濊禆锛?3. 鐢?pip 鎴?uv 瀹夎椤圭洰渚濊禆

```powershell
# 1. 鍒涘缓 Conda 鐜锛堟寚瀹?Python 鐗堟湰锛?conda create -n clonecut python=3.10.18 -y
conda activate clonecut

# 2. 瀹夎 FFmpeg锛堢郴缁熺骇渚濊禆锛?conda install -c conda-forge ffmpeg -y

# 3a. 鐢?Pip 瀹夎椤圭洰渚濊禆
pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]"

# 鎴栬€?3b. 鐢?UV 瀹夎椤圭洰渚濊禆锛堟洿蹇級
# pip install uv
# uv pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]"

# 鏇存柊渚濊禆锛堝鏋滈渶瑕侊級
# pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]" --upgrade
# 鎴栬€?# uv pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]" --upgrade
```

### 鏂规硶涓夛細浣跨敤 Pip

1. 瀹夎 Python 3.10.18锛堟帹鑽愮増鏈級
2. 瀹夎 FFmpeg锛堢郴缁熺骇渚濊禆锛?   - Windows: 浠庡畼缃戜笅杞藉苟娣诲姞鍒扮幆澧冨彉閲?   - Linux: `sudo apt-get install ffmpeg`
   - macOS: `brew install ffmpeg`

```powershell
# 鍒涘缓铏氭嫙鐜
python -m venv venv

# 婵€娲昏櫄鎷熺幆澧冿紙Windows锛?venv\Scripts\activate

# 婵€娲昏櫄鎷熺幆澧冿紙Linux/macOS锛?# source venv/bin/activate

# 瀹夎椤圭洰鍙婂叾鎵€鏈変緷璧?pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]"

# 鏇存柊渚濊禆锛堝鏋滈渶瑕侊級
pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]" --upgrade
```

### 鏂规硶鍥涳細浣跨敤 UV锛堟帹鑽愶紝鏇村揩锛?
1. 瀹夎 UV锛歚pip install uv`
2. 瀹夎 FFmpeg锛堢郴缁熺骇渚濊禆锛?   - Windows: 浠庡畼缃戜笅杞藉苟娣诲姞鍒扮幆澧冨彉閲?   - Linux: `sudo apt-get install ffmpeg`
   - macOS: `brew install ffmpeg`

```powershell
# 浣跨敤 UV 鍒涘缓铏氭嫙鐜骞跺畨瑁呬緷璧?uv venv
uv pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]"

# 婵€娲昏櫄鎷熺幆澧冿紙Windows锛?.venv\Scripts\activate

# 婵€娲昏櫄鎷熺幆澧冿紙Linux/macOS锛?# source .venv/bin/activate

# 鏇存柊渚濊禆锛堝鏋滈渶瑕侊級
uv pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]" --upgrade
```

## 2. 閰嶇疆鍑嗗

```powershell
Copy-Item config/settings.yaml.example config/settings.yaml
Copy-Item config/forum_settings.yaml.example config/forum_settings.yaml
Copy-Item machines.txt.example machines.txt
```

## 3. 鍚姩鏈嶅姟锛堟帹鑽愮敤鑴氭湰锛?
鍏堝惎鍔?Worker锛屽啀鍚姩 Monitor銆?
### 浣跨敤 Conda 鐜锛堟柟娉曚竴鎴栨柟娉曚簩锛?
```powershell
conda activate clonecut
.\scripts\2-鍚姩宸ヤ綔鑺傜偣.bat
.\scripts\1-鍚姩鐩戞帶鑺傜偣.bat
```

### 浣跨敤 Pip 鎴?UV 鐜锛堟柟娉曚笁鎴栨柟娉曞洓锛?
```powershell
# 浣跨敤 Pip 鐜
# 婵€娲昏櫄鎷熺幆澧?venv\Scripts\activate

# 浣跨敤 UV 鐜
# 婵€娲昏櫄鎷熺幆澧?# .venv\Scripts\activate

.\scripts\2-鍚姩宸ヤ綔鑺傜偣.bat
.\scripts\1-鍚姩鐩戞帶鑺傜偣.bat
```

榛樿鍦板潃锛?
- Monitor: `http://127.0.0.1:8200`
- Worker: `http://127.0.0.1:8205`

濡傛灉浣犳湰鏈虹鍙ｅ啿绐侊紝鐢ㄩ珮绔彛鑱旇皟锛?
```powershell
.\scripts\3-楂樼鍙ｈ仈璋冨惎鍔?bat
```

楂樼鍙ｅ湴鍧€锛?
- Monitor: `http://127.0.0.1:18200`
- Worker: `http://127.0.0.1:18205`

## 4. 鎻愪氦浠诲姟锛堣棰戠ず渚嬶級

鎶?`source_url` 鏀规垚浣犺嚜宸辩殑瑙嗛璺緞銆?
```powershell
$monitorUrl = "http://127.0.0.1:8200"   # 楂樼鍙ｈ仈璋冩椂鏀规垚 http://127.0.0.1:18200

$body = @{
  task_type  = "video"
  source_url = "D:/clonecut/input/demo.mp4"
  priority   = "normal"
  metadata   = @{ source = "manual" }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

$resp = Invoke-RestMethod -Method Post `
  -Uri "$monitorUrl/api/tasks/submit" `
  -ContentType "application/json" `
  -Body $body

$resp
```

杩斿洖閲屼細鏈?`task_id`銆?
## 5. 鏌ヨ浠诲姟鐘舵€?
鎶?`<task_id>` 鎹㈡垚浣犵殑浠诲姟 ID銆?
```powershell
$workerUrl = "http://127.0.0.1:8205"   # 楂樼鍙ｈ仈璋冩椂鏀规垚 http://127.0.0.1:18205

Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$workerUrl/api/worker/status"
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$workerUrl/api/worker/task/<task_id>"
```

甯歌鐘舵€侊紙绠€鍖栵級锛歚pending` -> `processing` -> `completed/failed`

瀹屾暣鐘舵€佸彲鑳藉寘鍚細`pending` -> `downloading` -> `processing` -> `uploading` -> `completed/failed`锛屼篃鍙兘鏄?`cancelled`銆?
## 6. 鏌ョ湅杈撳嚭缁撴灉

榛樿杈撳嚭鐩綍鍦細

- `D:/clonecut/output`

浣犱篃鍙互鍦?`config/settings.yaml` 鐨?`paths.output_dir` 鑷畾涔夈€?
## 7. 涓€閿啋鐑熼獙璇侊紙鎺ㄨ崘锛?
娉ㄦ剰锛歚smoke_local.py` 浼氳嚜琛屾媺璧?monitor/worker 杩涚▼銆備笉瑕佸拰绗?3 姝ュ悓绔彛骞惰杩愯锛涘鏋滅鍙ｅ凡琚崰鐢紝璇锋崲涓€缁勭┖闂茬鍙ｃ€?
```powershell
python tests/smoke/smoke_local.py --monitor-port 18300 --worker-port 18305
```

鐪嬪埌 `SMOKE OK` 璇存槑绀轰緥閰嶇疆涓嬬殑鍩虹閾捐矾鍙敤锛涗笉绛夊悓浜庝綘褰撳墠 `config/settings.yaml` 鍜?`machines.txt` 宸插叏閮ㄩ獙璇併€?
## 8. 甯哥敤鎺ュ彛閫熸煡

- Monitor 鍋ュ悍妫€鏌ワ細`GET /health`
- 鎻愪氦浠诲姟锛歚POST /api/tasks/submit`
- 鏌ョ湅寰呭垎鍙戯細`GET /api/tasks/pending`
- 鎵嬪姩鍒嗗彂涓€娆★細`POST /api/dispatch-once`
- Worker 鐘舵€侊細`GET /api/worker/status`
- Worker 浠诲姟璇︽儏锛歚GET /api/worker/task/<task_id>`

## 9. 鏈€缁堥獙鏀惰剼鏈紙鐙珛鎵ц锛?
浠ヤ笅鑴氭湰鍧囧彧浣跨敤 `clonecut` 褰撳墠鐩綍閰嶇疆涓庣幆澧冨彉閲忥紝涓嶄緷璧栨棫椤圭洰鐩綍锛?
```powershell
$env:FORUM_BASE_URL = "https://your.forum.host"
$env:FORUM_TARGET_URL = "https://your.forum.host/forum-2-1.html"
$env:FORUM_USERNAME = "your_forum_user"
$env:FORUM_PASSWORD = "your_forum_password"

# R1: 璁哄潧瀹炵綉绔埌绔洖褰?python scripts/forum_e2e_regression.py --forum-config config/forum_settings.yaml --monitor-url http://127.0.0.1:18200 --worker-url http://127.0.0.1:18205 --cycles 3 --execute-reply --stop-on-failure

# R2: 璧勬簮鐩戞帶绮惧害涓庢€ц兘鍘嬫祴
python scripts/resource_monitor_benchmark.py --monitor-interval-seconds 1.0 --sample-interval-seconds 1.0 --cpu-workers 2 --memory-load-mb 512

# R3: nightly 澶辫触鍥炵亴涓庨暱鏈熻秼鍔?python scripts/regression_nightly_backfill.py --report-json reports/regression/baseline_nightly.json --gate-json reports/regression/b1_gate_nightly.json --source-manifest data/regression/manifest/samples_v2_30.json --failed-manifest data/regression/manifest/samples_failed_backfill.json --history-jsonl reports/regression/nightly_history.jsonl --summary-markdown reports/regression/nightly_backfill_summary.md
```

[project.optional-dependencies]

# 闃舵4锛氳鍧涚埇铏紙Playwright 椹卞姩锛?forum = [

# 闃舵6锛欸PU 鐩戞帶锛堝彲閫夛級

# 寮€鍙戞祴璇?test = [
  "pytest>=8.0",
  "pytest-timeout>=2.0",
  "pytest-asyncio>=0.24",

[project.scripts]
clonecut-monitor = "apps.monitor.main:main"
clonecut-worker  = "apps.worker.main:main"

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
python_files = ["test_*.py"]

[tool.setuptools]
include-package-data = true

[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
where = ["."]
include = ["apps*", "adapters*", "domain*", "infra*", "shared*"]
exclude = [
  "tests*",
  "docs*",
  "data*",
  "reports*",
  "input*",
  "output*",
  "logs*",
  "config*",
  "scripts*",
]

Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #28 commentary 02:17:51

我已经读完文件，发现 README.md 正常，但 如何使用.md 和 pyproject.toml 出现明显乱码迹象。下一步我会验证这两个文件是否是编码问题还是语法问题，并检查 pyproject.toml 是否会影响 pip install -e。

Tool Call #31 shell_command call_2zchTJ4OSoMrDKKgHoO0IhXB 02:18:03
shell_command input={"command":"@'\nimport tomllib, pathlib\np = pathlib.Path('pyproject.toml')\ntry:\n data = tomllib.loads(p.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))\n print('PYPROJECT_PARSE_OK')\n print('de...
Show input payload 
{
  "command": "@'\nimport tomllib, pathlib\np = pathlib.Path('pyproject.toml')\ntry:\n    data = tomllib.loads(p.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))\n    print('PYPROJECT_PARSE_OK')\n    print('description=', data.get('project', {}).get('description'))\n    print('optional_groups=', sorted(list(data.get('project', {}).get('optional-dependencies', {}).keys())))\nexcept Exception as e:\n    print('PYPROJECT_PARSE_ERROR')\n    print(type(e).__name__, e)\n'@ | python -",
  "workdir": "d:\\clonecut"
}
Tool Call #32 shell_command call_ETGoWyAcrrhuclJZga1cCgvI 02:18:03
shell_command input={"command":"@'\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfiles = ['README.md', '如何使用.md', 'pyproject.toml']\nencs = ['utf-8', 'utf-8-sig', 'gbk', 'gb18030']\nfor f in files:\n b = Path(f).read...
Show input payload 
{
  "command": "@'\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfiles = ['README.md', '如何使用.md', 'pyproject.toml']\nencs = ['utf-8', 'utf-8-sig', 'gbk', 'gb18030']\nfor f in files:\n    b = Path(f).read_bytes()\n    print(f'FILE {f} bytes={len(b)}')\n    for enc in encs:\n        try:\n            t = b.decode(enc)\n            sample = t[:80].replace('\\n', ' ')\n            print(f'  {enc}: OK sample={sample!r}')\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f'  {enc}: FAIL {e.__class__.__name__}: {e}')\n    print()\n'@ | python -",
  "workdir": "d:\\clonecut"
}

    "limit": 10000

Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.3 seconds
Output:
   1: # CloneCut
   2: 
   3: CloneCut 是面向生产的任务处理平台骨架项目，目标是平滑承接现有 FunClip/TTS 生产链路，并支持后续模块化扩展。
   4: 
   5: ## 当前状态
   6: 
   7: - 已完成目录分层骨架
   8: - 已完成开发基线文档
   9: - 已提供配置模板和启动脚本模板
  10: - 已提供统一任务模型与配置加载基础代码
  11: - 已接入阶段1增量能力：Redis/SQLite（可用即启用，不可用自动回退内存）
  12: - 已接入阶段2增量能力：内置 `VideoPipeline`（stage0~9）与 ASR/剪辑基础实现
  13: - 已提供真实样本回归对比与 B1 门槛判定脚本：`scripts/regression_compare.py`、`scripts/regression_gate.py`
  14: - 已提供 nightly 回归门槛 workflow：`.github/workflows/nightly-regression-gate.yml`
  15: - 已补最终验收闭环脚本：`scripts/forum_e2e_regression.py`、`scripts/resource_monitor_benchmark.py`、`scripts/regression_nightly_backfill.py`
  16: - `use_internal_pipeline` 默认已切为 `true`（B1 达标后执行）
  17: - 独立态 30 样本回归已达标：`reports/regression/baseline_v30_independent_20260226.md` / `reports/regression/b1_gate_v30_independent_20260226.md`
  18: - 核心目标已达成，最终验收仍有 3 项待收口（论坛实网回归/监控压测/回归扩样）
  19: - 推荐运行环境：`conda` 环境 `clonecut`（Python `3.10.x`）
  20: 
  21: ## 快速开始（骨架阶段）
  22: 
  23: 0. 准备环境（CloneCut 独立环境）
  24: - `conda env create -f environment.yml`
  25: - `conda activate clonecut`
  26: - 已有 `clonecut` 环境请执行：`conda env update -n clonecut -f environment.yml --prune`
  27: 
  28: 1. 复制配置模板
  29: - `config/settings.yaml.example` -> `config/settings.yaml`
  30: - `config/forum_settings.yaml.example` -> `config/forum_settings.yaml`
  31: 
  32: 2. 启动监控节点（示例）
  33: - `scripts/1-启动监控节点.bat`
  34: 
  35: 3. 启动工作节点（示例）
  36: - `scripts/2-启动工作节点.bat`
  37: 
  38: 4. 高端口联调（防冲突）
  39: - `scripts/3-高端口联调启动.bat`
  40: - `scripts/4-运行冒烟测试.bat`
  41: - `scripts/5-灰度回滚停服.bat`
  42: 
  43: ## 关键文档
  44: 
  45: - `docs/开发文档.md`
  46: - `docs/任务模型说明.md`
  47: - `docs/配置规范.md`
  48: - `docs/独立化实施计划.md`
  49: - `docs/测试验证说明.md`
  50: - `docs/真实样本验收执行清单.md`
  51: - `docs/真实样本验收报告模板.md`
  52: - `docs/regression_samples_manifest.example.json`
  53: - `docs/文档二次评审-2026-02-25.md`
  54: 
  55: ## 本地高端口联调
  56: 
  57: ```powershell
  58: python tests/smoke/smoke_local.py --monitor-port 18200 --worker-port 18205
  59: ```
  60: 
  61: ```powershell
  62: python tests/smoke/smoke_local.py --monitor-port 18200 --worker-port 18205 --enable-tts --voice-name 你的可用音色
  63: ```
  64: 
  65: ## 回归门槛（B1）
  66: 
  67: ```powershell
  68: python scripts/regression_compare.py `
  69:   --legacy-dir data/regression/legacy `
  70:   --clonecut-dir data/regression/clonecut `
  71:   --manifest data/regression/manifest/samples_v2_30.json `
  72:   --output-json reports/regression/baseline_v30.json `
  73:   --output-markdown reports/regression/baseline_v30.md `
  74:   --duration-tolerance-seconds 1.0 `
  75:   --frame-tolerance 24 `
  76:   --subtitle-cue-tolerance 2 `
  77:   --subtitle-line-tolerance 4 `
  78:   --audio-duration-tolerance-seconds 1.0
  79: ```
  80: 
  81: ```powershell
  82: python scripts/regression_gate.py `
  83:   --report reports/regression/baseline_v30.json `
  84:   --output-json reports/regression/b1_gate_v30.json `
  85:   --output-markdown reports/regression/b1_gate_v30.md `
  86:   --min-samples 30 `
  87:   --min-pass-rate 0.9 `
  88:   --max-subtitle-presence-mismatch-ratio 0.05 `
  89:   --max-duration-frame-exceed-ratio 0.10 `
  90:   --max-fallback-used-ratio 0.10
  91: ```
  92: 
  93: ## 最终验收收口脚本（独立执行）
  94: 
  95: - 论坛实网端到端回归（发现 -> 下载 -> 处理 -> 回帖/附件上传）  
  96:   使用 `clonecut` 自身配置/环境变量，不依赖旧项目路径：
  97: 
  98: ```powershell
  99: $env:FORUM_BASE_URL = "https://your.forum.host"
 100: $env:FORUM_TARGET_URL = "https://your.forum.host/forum-2-1.html"
 101: $env:FORUM_USERNAME = "your_forum_user"
 102: $env:FORUM_PASSWORD = "your_forum_password"
 103: ```
 104: 
 105: ```powershell
 106: python scripts/forum_e2e_regression.py `
 107:   --forum-config config/forum_settings.yaml `
 108:   --monitor-url http://127.0.0.1:18200 `
 109:   --worker-url http://127.0.0.1:18205 `
 110:   --cycles 3 `
 111:   --execute-reply `
 112:   --stop-on-failure
 113: ```
 114: 
 115: - 资源监控精度与性能压测（CPU/内存/GPU）：
 116: 
 117: ```powershell
 118: python scripts/resource_monitor_benchmark.py `
 119:   --monitor-interval-seconds 1.0 `
 120:   --sample-interval-seconds 1.0 `
 121:   --cpu-workers 2 `
 122:   --memory-load-mb 512
 123: ```
 124: 
 125: - nightly 失败回灌与长期趋势：
 126: 
 127: ```powershell
 128: python scripts/regression_nightly_backfill.py `
 129:   --report-json reports/regression/baseline_nightly.json `
 130:   --gate-json reports/regression/b1_gate_nightly.json `
 131:   --source-manifest data/regression/manifest/samples_v2_30.json `
 132:   --failed-manifest data/regression/manifest/samples_failed_backfill.json `
 133:   --history-jsonl reports/regression/nightly_history.jsonl `
 134:   --summary-markdown reports/regression/nightly_backfill_summary.md
 135: ```
 136: 
 137: ## 可选执行引擎
 138: 
 139: - TTS/音色克隆内置引擎（默认开启）：`CLONECUT_TTS_ENABLE=true`
 140: - TTS 引擎选择：`CLONECUT_TTS_ENGINE=local|http|auto`（默认 `local`）
 141: - 缺省音色补全（可选）：`CLONECUT_TTS_DEFAULT_VOICE=...`
 142: - 外部视频命令桥接（默认关闭）：`CLONECUT_VIDEO_COMMAND_TEMPLATE=...`
 143: - stage6 可选时长引导：`CLONECUT_STAGE6_DURATION_GUIDE_PATH=...`
 144: - 扩样清单（30样本）：`data/regression/manifest/samples_v2_30.json`
 145: 
 146: ### 内置链路说明（2026-02-26）
 147: 
 148: - 目标：不依赖旧项目运行时，保持 CloneCut 内置链路可独立运行。
 149: - 行为：
 150:   - `stage4_asr` 只使用 `faster_whisper` / sidecar / timeline fallback；
 151:   - `stage6_clip` 支持文本匹配剪辑与时长引导：
 152:     - 时长引导：`CLONECUT_STAGE6_DURATION_GUIDE_PATH=...`
 153:     - 文本匹配剪辑：`CLONECUT_STAGE6_CLIP_TEXT_MATCH=true`
 154:   - 其余 stage 继续走新架构链路。
 155:   - 严格等价模式：`CLONECUT_STRICT_EQUIVALENCE=true` 时禁止 stage2/4/6/7 的 copy/timeline fallback。
 156: - 验证结果：`python -m pytest -q` -> `72 passed, 1 skipped`（2026-02-26 本地校验）；30 样本独立态回归 `baseline_v30_independent_20260226` 为 `100%`，对应 B1 `b1_gate_v30_independent_20260226` 为 `PASS`。
 157: 
 158: ## 生产并行运行建议
 159: 
 160: - 旧系统：`8000/8005`
 161: - CloneCut：`8200/8205`
 162: - Redis 前缀：`clonecut_monitor:`、`clonecut_worker:`
 163:

Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #95 commentary 02:18:51

补充确认到一个关键点：README.md 有 BOM，另外两个文件可能是无 BOM 的 UTF-8，导致 PowerShell 默认按 ANSI 读成乱码。现在我会用 -Encoding UTF8 重新读取，再判断是否真的有语法错误。

Tool Call #96 shell_command call_H7foNAEf6sX6Thp7AoikCQT6 02:18:51

   1: [build-system]
   2: requires = ["setuptools>=68", "wheel"]

   4: 
   5: [project]
   6: name = "clonecut"
   7: version = "2.0.0"
   8: description = "CloneCut — 独立化视频处理平台"
   9: requires-python = ">=3.10,<3.11"
  10: dependencies = [
  11:   # 基础
  12:   "pyyaml>=6.0",
  13:   "flask>=3.0",
  14:   "waitress>=3.0",
  15:   "fastapi>=0.100",
  16:   "uvicorn>=0.20",
  17:   "requests>=2.31",
  18:   "python-dotenv>=1.0",
  19: 
  20:   # 阶段1：任务持久化
  21:   "redis>=5.0",
  22: 
  23:   # 阶段2：视频流水线
  24:   "auto-editor>=24.0",
  25:   "Pillow>=10.0",
  26:   "openai>=1.0",
  27:   "pysrt>=1.1",
  28:   "pysubs2>=1.6",
  29:   "moviepy>=1.0",
  30: 
  31:   # 阶段4：论坛热词
  32:   "jieba>=0.42",
  33: 
  34:   # 阶段6：监控报告
  35:   "psutil>=5.9",
  36: ]
  37: 
  38: [project.optional-dependencies]
  39: # 阶段2：ASR 引擎（GPU 环境安装）
  40: asr = [
  41:   "funasr>=1.0",
  42:   "torch>=2.0",
  43:   "torchaudio>=2.0",
  44:   "modelscope>=1.0",
  45:   "faster-whisper>=1.0",
  46: ]
  47: 
  48: # 阶段2/5：LLM 引擎（按需安装）
  49: llm = [
  50:   "dashscope>=1.0",
  51:   "g4f>=0.3",
  52: ]
  53: 
  54: # 阶段4：论坛爬虫（Playwright 驱动）
  55: forum = [
  56:   "playwright>=1.40",
  57:   "beautifulsoup4>=4.12",
  58: ]
  59: 
  60: # 阶段6：GPU 监控（可选）
  61: gpu = [
  62:   "GPUtil>=1.4",
  63: ]
  64: 
  65: # 开发测试
  66: test = [
  67:   "pytest>=8.0",
  68:   "pytest-timeout>=2.0",
  69:   "pytest-asyncio>=0.24",
  70: ]
  71: 
  72: [project.scripts]
  73: clonecut-monitor = "apps.monitor.main:main"
  74: clonecut-worker  = "apps.worker.main:main"
  75: 
  76: [tool.pytest.ini_options]
  77: testpaths = ["tests"]
  78: python_files = ["test_*.py"]
  79: 
  80: [tool.setuptools]
  81: include-package-data = true
  82: 
  83: [tool.setuptools.packages.find]
  84: where = ["."]
  85: include = ["apps*", "adapters*", "domain*", "infra*", "shared*"]
  86: exclude = [
  87:   "tests*",
  88:   "docs*",
  89:   "data*",
  90:   "reports*",
  91:   "input*",
  92:   "output*",
  93:   "logs*",
  94:   "config*",
  95:   "scripts*",
  96: ]
Tool Result #100 shell_command call_ujMPl0FaidNFipWMDzbKzdG2 exit 0 02:18:51
shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.3 seconds Output: 1: # 如何使用 2: 3: 本文给你一套从 0 到 1 的最短使用流程，按顺序执行即可。 4: 5: ## 1. 环境准备 6: 7: ### 方法一：使用 Conda（推荐） 8: 9: ```powershell 10: # 注意：如果你已经有 clonec...
Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.3 seconds
Output:
   1: # 如何使用
   2: 
   3: 本文给你一套从 0 到 1 的最短使用流程，按顺序执行即可。
   4: 
   5: ## 1. 环境准备
   6: 
   7: ### 方法一：使用 Conda（推荐）
   8: 
   9: ```powershell
  10: # 注意：如果你已经有 clonecut 环境，可以跳过创建，直接激活
  11: conda env create -f environment.yml
  12: conda activate clonecut
  13: # 已有环境请更新到最新依赖
  14: conda env update -n clonecut -f environment.yml --prune
  15: ```
  16: 
  17: ### 方法二：自己创建 Conda 环境 + Pip/UV
  18: 
  19: 如果你习惯自己创建 Conda 环境，可以用这种方法：
  20: 
  21: 1. 创建并激活 Conda 环境
  22: 2. 安装 FFmpeg（系统级依赖）
  23: 3. 用 pip 或 uv 安装项目依赖
  24: 
  25: ```powershell
  26: # 1. 创建 Conda 环境（指定 Python 版本）
  27: conda create -n clonecut python=3.10.18 -y
  28: conda activate clonecut
  29: 
  30: # 2. 安装 FFmpeg（系统级依赖）
  31: conda install -c conda-forge ffmpeg -y
  32: 
  33: # 3a. 用 Pip 安装项目依赖
  34: pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]"
  35: 
  36: # 或者 3b. 用 UV 安装项目依赖（更快）
  37: # pip install uv
  38: # uv pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]"
  39: 
  40: # 更新依赖（如果需要）
  41: # pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]" --upgrade
  42: # 或者
  43: # uv pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]" --upgrade
  44: ```
  45: 
  46: ### 方法三：使用 Pip
  47: 
  48: 1. 安装 Python 3.10.18（推荐版本）
  49: 2. 安装 FFmpeg（系统级依赖）
  50:    - Windows: 从官网下载并添加到环境变量
  51:    - Linux: `sudo apt-get install ffmpeg`
  52:    - macOS: `brew install ffmpeg`
  53: 
  54: ```powershell
  55: # 创建虚拟环境
  56: python -m venv venv
  57: 
  58: # 激活虚拟环境（Windows）
  59: venv\Scripts\activate
  60: 
  61: # 激活虚拟环境（Linux/macOS）
  62: # source venv/bin/activate
  63: 
  64: # 安装项目及其所有依赖
  65: pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]"
  66: 
  67: # 更新依赖（如果需要）
  68: pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]" --upgrade
  69: ```
  70: 
  71: ### 方法四：使用 UV（推荐，更快）
  72: 
  73: 1. 安装 UV：`pip install uv`
  74: 2. 安装 FFmpeg（系统级依赖）
  75:    - Windows: 从官网下载并添加到环境变量
  76:    - Linux: `sudo apt-get install ffmpeg`
  77:    - macOS: `brew install ffmpeg`
  78: 
  79: ```powershell
  80: # 使用 UV 创建虚拟环境并安装依赖
  81: uv venv
  82: uv pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]"
  83: 
  84: # 激活虚拟环境（Windows）
  85: .venv\Scripts\activate
  86: 
  87: # 激活虚拟环境（Linux/macOS）
  88: # source .venv/bin/activate
  89: 
  90: # 更新依赖（如果需要）
  91: uv pip install -e ".[asr,llm,forum,test]" --upgrade
  92: ```
  93: 
  94: ## 2. 配置准备
  95: 
  96: ```powershell
  97: Copy-Item config/settings.yaml.example config/settings.yaml
  98: Copy-Item config/forum_settings.yaml.example config/forum_settings.yaml
  99: Copy-Item machines.txt.example machines.txt
 100: ```
 101: 
 102: ## 3. 启动服务（推荐用脚本）
 103: 
 104: 先启动 Worker，再启动 Monitor。
 105: 
 106: ### 使用 Conda 环境（方法一或方法二）
 107: 
 108: ```powershell
 109: conda activate clonecut
 110: .\scripts\2-启动工作节点.bat
 111: .\scripts\1-启动监控节点.bat
 112: ```
 113: 
 114: ### 使用 Pip 或 UV 环境（方法三或方法四）
 115: 
 116: ```powershell
 117: # 使用 Pip 环境
 118: # 激活虚拟环境
 119: venv\Scripts\activate
 120: 
 121: # 使用 UV 环境
 122: # 激活虚拟环境
 123: # .venv\Scripts\activate
 124: 
 125: .\scripts\2-启动工作节点.bat
 126: .\scripts\1-启动监控节点.bat
 127: ```
 128: 
 129: 默认地址：
 130: 
 131: - Monitor: `http://127.0.0.1:8200`
 132: - Worker: `http://127.0.0.1:8205`
 133: 
 134: 如果你本机端口冲突，用高端口联调：
 135: 
 136: ```powershell
 137: .\scripts\3-高端口联调启动.bat
 138: ```
 139: 
 140: 高端口地址：
 141: 
 142: - Monitor: `http://127.0.0.1:18200`
 143: - Worker: `http://127.0.0.1:18205`
 144: 
 145: ## 4. 提交任务（视频示例）
 146: 
 147: 把 `source_url` 改成你自己的视频路径。
 148: 
 149: ```powershell
 150: $monitorUrl = "http://127.0.0.1:8200"   # 高端口联调时改成 http://127.0.0.1:18200
 151: 
 152: $body = @{
 153:   task_type  = "video"
 154:   source_url = "D:/clonecut/input/demo.mp4"
 155:   priority   = "normal"
 156:   metadata   = @{ source = "manual" }
 157: } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5
 158: 
 159: $resp = Invoke-RestMethod -Method Post `
 160:   -Uri "$monitorUrl/api/tasks/submit" `
 161:   -ContentType "application/json" `
 162:   -Body $body
 163: 
 164: $resp
 165: ```
 166: 
 167: 返回里会有 `task_id`。
 168: 
 169: ## 5. 查询任务状态
 170: 
 171: 把 `<task_id>` 换成你的任务 ID。
 172: 
 173: ```powershell
 174: $workerUrl = "http://127.0.0.1:8205"   # 高端口联调时改成 http://127.0.0.1:18205
 175: 
 176: Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$workerUrl/api/worker/status"
 177: Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$workerUrl/api/worker/task/<task_id>"
 178: ```
 179: 
 180: 常见状态（简化）：`pending` -> `processing` -> `completed/failed`
 181: 
 182: 完整状态可能包含：`pending` -> `downloading` -> `processing` -> `uploading` -> `completed/failed`，也可能是 `cancelled`。
 183: 
 184: ## 6. 查看输出结果
 185: 
 186: 默认输出目录在：
 187: 
 188: - `D:/clonecut/output`
 189: 
 190: 你也可以在 `config/settings.yaml` 的 `paths.output_dir` 自定义。
 191: 
 192: ## 7. 一键冒烟验证（推荐）
 193: 
 194: 注意：`smoke_local.py` 会自行拉起 monitor/worker 进程。不要和第 3 步同端口并行运行；如果端口已被占用，请换一组空闲端口。
 195: 
 196: ```powershell
 197: python tests/smoke/smoke_local.py --monitor-port 18300 --worker-port 18305
 198: ```
 199: 
 200: 看到 `SMOKE OK` 说明示例配置下的基础链路可用；不等同于你当前 `config/settings.yaml` 和 `machines.txt` 已全部验证。
 201: 
 202: ## 8. 常用接口速查
 203: 
 204: - Monitor 健康检查：`GET /health`
 205: - 提交任务：`POST /api/tasks/submit`
 206: - 查看待分发：`GET /api/tasks/pending`
 207: - 手动分发一次：`POST /api/dispatch-once`
 208: - Worker 状态：`GET /api/worker/status`
 209: - Worker 任务详情：`GET /api/worker/task/<task_id>`
 210: 
 211: ## 9. 最终验收脚本（独立执行）
 212: 
 213: 以下脚本均只使用 `clonecut` 当前目录配置与环境变量，不依赖旧项目目录：
 214: 
 215: ```powershell
 216: $env:FORUM_BASE_URL = "https://your.forum.host"
 217: $env:FORUM_TARGET_URL = "https://your.forum.host/forum-2-1.html"
 218: $env:FORUM_USERNAME = "your_forum_user"
 219: $env:FORUM_PASSWORD = "your_forum_password"
 220: 
 221: # R1: 论坛实网端到端回归
 222: python scripts/forum_e2e_regression.py --forum-config config/forum_settings.yaml --monitor-url http://127.0.0.1:18200 --worker-url http://127.0.0.1:18205 --cycles 3 --execute-reply --stop-on-failure
 223: 
 224: # R2: 资源监控精度与性能压测
 225: python scripts/resource_monitor_benchmark.py --monitor-interval-seconds 1.0 --sample-interval-seconds 1.0 --cpu-workers 2 --memory-load-mb 512
 226: 
 227: # R3: nightly 失败回灌与长期趋势
 228: python scripts/regression_nightly_backfill.py --report-json reports/regression/baseline_nightly.json --gate-json reports/regression/b1_gate_nightly.json --source-manifest data/regression/manifest/samples_v2_30.json --failed-manifest data/regression/manifest/samples_failed_backfill.json --history-jsonl reports/regression/nightly_history.jsonl --summary-markdown reports/regression/nightly_backfill_summary.md
 229: ```

(file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",

    "limit": 10000

Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIf a decision is necessary and cannot be discovered from local context, ask the user directly. However, in Default mode you should strongly prefer executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions.\r\n"
    }
  },

  "summary": "auto",
  "user_instructions": "## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",

    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.3 seconds
Output:
   1: @echo off
   2: setlocal EnableExtensions
   3: chcp 65001 >nul 2>&1
   4: 
   5: set ROOT=%~dp0\..
   6: cd /d %ROOT%
   7: 
   8: echo 127.0.0.1:18205:1> machines.highport.txt
   9: 
  10: echo [1/2] start worker on 18205
  11: start "clonecut-worker-18205" cmd /k "cd /d %ROOT% && set CLONECUT_ROLE=worker && set CLONECUT_PORT=18205 && set REDIS_NAMESPACE=clonecut_worker_test: && set CLONECUT_TTS_ENABLE=false && python -m apps.worker.main --config config/settings.yaml --host 127.0.0.1 --port 18205"
  12: 
  13: timeout /t 2 /nobreak >nul
  14: 
  15: echo [2/2] start monitor on 18200
  16: start "clonecut-monitor-18200" cmd /k "cd /d %ROOT% && set CLONECUT_ROLE=monitor && set CLONECUT_PORT=18200 && set REDIS_NAMESPACE=clonecut_monitor_test: && python -m apps.monitor.main --config config/settings.yaml --host 127.0.0.1 --port 18200 --machines-file machines.highport.txt"
  17: 
  18: echo clonecut high-port services started
  19: echo monitor: http://127.0.0.1:18200
  20: echo worker:  http://127.0.0.1:18205
  21: pause

Tool Result #157 shell_command call_eQE27AT2SZoYwDQGI8UCJbd9 exit 0 02:20:06
shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.3 seconds Output: 1: @echo off 2: setlocal EnableExtensions 3: chcp 65001 >nul 2>&1 4: 5: echo stopping clonecut ports: 18200 18205 8200 8205 6: for %%...
Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.3 seconds
Output:
   1: @echo off
   2: setlocal EnableExtensions
   3: chcp 65001 >nul 2>&1
   4: 
   5: echo stopping clonecut ports: 18200 18205 8200 8205
   6: for %%P in (18200 18205 8200 8205) do (
   7:   for /f "tokens=5" %%I in ('netstat -ano ^| findstr :%%P ^| findstr LISTENING') do (
   8:     echo stop port %%P pid %%I
   9:     taskkill /PID %%I /F >nul 2>&1
  10:   )
  11: )
  12: 
  13: echo done
  14: pause

Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). Output:
   1: from __future__ import annotations
   2: 
   3: from dataclasses import dataclass
   4: import os
   5: from pathlib import Path
   6: import re
   7: from typing import List, Optional, Tuple
   8: 
   9: from domain.video.stages.base import StageResult
  10: from domain.video.stages.utils import (
  11:     build_simple_srt_segments,
  12:     copy_video,
  13:     probe_duration_seconds,
  14:     stage_artifact_path,
  15:     stage_video_path,
  16:     write_json,
  17:     write_srt,
  18:     write_text,
  19: )
  20: 
  21: SrtSegment = Tuple[float, float, str]
  22: _TIMECODE_RE = re.compile(r"(?P<h>\d{2}):(?P<m>\d{2}):(?P<s>\d{2})[,.](?P<ms>\d{3})")
  23: 
  24: 
  25: @dataclass
  26: class AsrAttempt:
  27:     backend: str
  28:     engine: str
  29:     detail: str
  30:     segment_count: int
  31: 
  32: 
  33: def run(input_video: str, output_dir: str, task_id: str) -> StageResult:
  34:     source = Path(input_video)
  35:     if not source.exists():
  36:         return StageResult(success=False, error_message=f"input video not found: {input_video}")
  37: 
  38:     target = stage_video_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage4_asr")
  39:     copy_video(str(source), target)
  40: 
  41:     srt_path = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage4_asr_subtitles", ".srt")
  42:     transcript_path = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage4_asr_transcript", ".txt")
  43:     manifest_path = stage_artifact_path(output_dir, task_id, "stage4_asr_manifest", ".json")
  44: 
  45:     requested_backend = _normalize_backend(os.getenv("CLONECUT_ASR_BACKEND", "auto"))
  46:     prefer_sidecar = _env_bool("CLONECUT_ASR_PREFER_SIDECAR", False)
  47:     min_segments = _env_int("CLONECUT_ASR_MIN_SEGMENTS", default=1, min_value=1)
  48:     strict_mode = _env_bool("CLONECUT_STRICT_EQUIVALENCE", False)
  49:     duration = probe_duration_seconds(str(source))
  50: 
  51:     segments, engine, detail, attempts = _resolve_segments(
  52:         source=source,
  53:         duration_seconds=duration,
  54:         requested_backend=requested_backend,
  55:         prefer_sidecar=prefer_sidecar,
  56:         min_segments=min_segments,
  57:     )
  58:     if not segments:
  59:         segments = build_simple_srt_segments(duration_seconds=duration, step_seconds=4.0)
  60:         engine = "timeline_fallback"
  61:         detail = "asr engine unavailable or no speech extracted"
  62:         attempts.append(
  63:             AsrAttempt(
  64:                 backend="timeline_fallback",
  65:                 engine="timeline_fallback",
  66:                 detail=detail,
  67:                 segment_count=len(segments),
  68:             )
  69:         )
  70: 
  71:     write_srt(srt_path, segments)
  72:     write_text(transcript_path, [text for _, _, text in segments])
  73:     first_engine = attempts[0].engine if attempts else engine
  74:     fallback_used = first_engine != engine or engine == "timeline_fallback"
  75:     strict_rejected = strict_mode and engine == "timeline_fallback"
  76:     write_json(
  77:         manifest_path,
  78:         {
  79:             "task_id": task_id,
  80:             "engine": engine,
  81:             "segment_count": len(segments),
  82:             "duration_seconds": duration,
  83:             "detail": detail,
  84:             "backend_requested": requested_backend,
  85:             "prefer_sidecar": prefer_sidecar,
  86:             "min_segments_required": min_segments,
  87:             "fallback_used": fallback_used,
  88:             "strict_mode": strict_mode,
  89:             "strict_rejected": strict_rejected,
  90:             "attempts": [
  91:                 {
  92:                     "backend": item.backend,
  93:                     "engine": item.engine,
  94:                     "segment_count": item.segment_count,
  95:                     "detail": item.detail,
  96:                 }
  97:                 for item in attempts
  98:             ],
  99:             "subtitle_path": str(srt_path),
 100:             "transcript_path": str(transcript_path),
 101:         },
 102:     )
 103: 
 104:     if strict_rejected:
 105:         return StageResult(
 106:             success=False,
 107:             error_message="stage4 strict equivalence reject: timeline fallback used",
 108:         )
 109: 
 110:     return StageResult(success=True, output_file=str(target))
 111: 
 112: 
 113: def _resolve_segments(
 114:     source: Path,
 115:     duration_seconds: float,
 116:     requested_backend: str,
 117:     prefer_sidecar: bool,
 118:     min_segments: int,
 119: ) -> Tuple[List[SrtSegment], str, str, List[AsrAttempt]]:
 120:     order = _backend_order(requested_backend=requested_backend, prefer_sidecar=prefer_sidecar)
 121:     attempts: List[AsrAttempt] = []
 122: 
 123:     for backend in order:
 124:         if backend == "faster_whisper":
 125:             segments, detail = _transcribe_with_faster_whisper(source)
 126:             attempts.append(
 127:                 AsrAttempt(
 128:                     backend=backend,
 129:                     engine="faster_whisper",
 130:                     detail=detail,
 131:                     segment_count=len(segments),
 132:                 )
 133:             )
 134:             if _segments_usable(segments, min_segments=min_segments):
 135:                 return segments, "faster_whisper", detail, attempts
 136:             continue
 137: 
 138:         if backend == "sidecar":
 139:             segments, engine, detail = _load_sidecar_segments(
 140:                 source=source,
 141:                 duration_seconds=duration_seconds,
 142:             )
 143:             attempts.append(
 144:                 AsrAttempt(
 145:                     backend=backend,
 146:                     engine=engine,
 147:                     detail=detail,
 148:                     segment_count=len(segments),
 149:                 )
 150:             )
 151:             if _segments_usable(segments, min_segments=min_segments):
 152:                 return segments, engine, detail, attempts
 153: 
 154:     if attempts:
 155:         return [], "timeline_fallback", attempts[-1].detail, attempts
 156:     return [], "timeline_fallback", "no asr backend enabled", attempts
 157: 
 158: 
 159: def _backend_order(requested_backend: str, prefer_sidecar: bool) -> List[str]:
 160:     if requested_backend == "faster_whisper":
 161:         return ["faster_whisper", "sidecar"]
 162:     if requested_backend == "sidecar":
 163:         return ["sidecar"]
 164:     if prefer_sidecar:
 165:         return ["sidecar", "faster_whisper"]
 166:     return ["faster_whisper", "sidecar"]
 167: 
 168: 
 169: def _normalize_backend(raw: str) -> str:
 170:     backend = (raw or "").strip().lower()
 171:     if backend in {"faster_whisper", "sidecar", "auto"}:
 172:         return backend
 173:     return "auto"
 174: 
 175: 
 176: def _segments_usable(segments: List[SrtSegment], min_segments: int) -> bool:
 177:     if len(segments) < min_segments:
 178:         return False
 179:     return any(bool(str(text).strip()) for _, _, text in segments)
 180: 
 181: 
 182: def _env_bool(name: str, default: bool) -> bool:
 183:     raw = os.getenv(name)
 184:     if raw is None:
 185:         return default
 186:     return raw.strip().lower() == "true"
 187: 
 188: 
 189: def _env_int(name: str, default: int, min_value: int = 0) -> int:
 190:     raw = os.getenv(name)
 191:     if raw is None:
 192:         return max(min_value, int(default))
 193:     try:
 194:         return max(min_value, int(raw))
 195:     except Exception:
 196:         return max(min_value, int(default))
 197: 
 198: 
 199: def _load_sidecar_segments(source: Path, duration_seconds: float) -> Tuple[List[SrtSegment], str, str]:
 200:     env_sidecar = os.getenv("CLONECUT_ASR_SIDECAR_PATH", "").strip()
 201:     candidates: List[Path] = []
 202:     if env_sidecar:
 203:         candidates.append(Path(env_sidecar))
 204:     candidates.extend(
 205:         [
 206:             source.with_suffix(".srt"),
 207:             source.with_suffix(".vtt"),
 208:             source.with_suffix(".txt"),
 209:         ]
 210:     )
 211: 
 212:     for sidecar in candidates:
 213:         if not sidecar.exists() or not sidecar.is_file():
 214:             continue
 215:         suffix = sidecar.suffix.lower()
 216:         raw = sidecar.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
 217:         if suffix in {".srt", ".vtt"}:
 218:             segments = _parse_timed_subtitle(raw)
 219:             if segments:
 220:                 return segments, f"sidecar_{suffix.lstrip('.')}", str(sidecar)
 221:         if suffix == ".txt":
 222:             segments = _build_segments_from_text(raw, duration_seconds)
 223:             if segments:
 224:                 return segments, "sidecar_txt", str(sidecar)
 225:     return [], "sidecar", "no sidecar transcript found"
 226: 
 227: 
 228: def _transcribe_with_faster_whisper(source: Path) -> Tuple[List[SrtSegment], str]:
 229:     model_size = os.getenv("CLONECUT_ASR_MODEL_SIZE", "small").strip() or "small"
 230:     language = os.getenv("CLONECUT_ASR_LANGUAGE", "").strip() or None
 231:     device = os.getenv("CLONECUT_ASR_DEVICE", "cpu").strip() or "cpu"
 232:     compute_type = os.getenv("CLONECUT_ASR_COMPUTE_TYPE", "int8").strip() or "int8"
 233:     try:
 234:         from faster_whisper import WhisperModel  # type: ignore
 235:     except Exception as exc:
 236:         return [], f"faster_whisper import failed: {exc}"
 237: 
 238:     try:
 239:         model = WhisperModel(model_size, device=device, compute_type=compute_type)
 240:         segment_iter, _ = model.transcribe(
 241:             str(source),
 242:             language=language,
 243:             vad_filter=True,
 244:             beam_size=5,
 245:         )
 246:         segments: List[SrtSegment] = []
 247:         for seg in segment_iter:
 248:             text = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", str(getattr(seg, "text", "") or "")).strip()
 249:             start = float(getattr(seg, "start", 0.0) or 0.0)
 250:             end = float(getattr(seg, "end", start) or start)
 251:             if not text:
 252:                 continue
 253:             if end <= start:
 254:                 end = start + 0.2
 255:             segments.append((start, end, text))
 256:         if not segments:
 257:             return [], "faster_whisper returned empty segments"
 258:         return segments, f"model={model_size}, language={language or 'auto'}"
 259:     except Exception as exc:
 260:         return [], f"faster_whisper transcribe failed: {exc}"
 261: 
 262: 
 263: def _parse_timed_subtitle(raw: str) -> List[SrtSegment]:
 264:     lines = raw.splitlines()
 265:     segments: List[SrtSegment] = []
 266:     idx = 0
 267:     while idx < len(lines):
 268:         line = lines[idx].strip()
 269:         idx += 1
 270:         if not line:
 271:             continue
 272:         if line.upper().startswith("WEBVTT"):
 273:             continue
 274:         if line.isdigit() and idx < len(lines):
 275:             line = lines[idx].strip()
 276:             idx += 1
 277:         if "-->" not in line:
 278:             continue
 279: 
 280:         start_token, end_token = [part.strip() for part in line.split("-->", 1)]
 281:         start = _parse_timecode(start_token.split(" ", 1)[0].strip())
 282:         end = _parse_timecode(end_token.split(" ", 1)[0].strip())
 283:         if start is None or end is None:
 284:             continue
 285:         if end <= start:
 286:             end = start + 0.2
 287: 
 288:         text_lines: List[str] = []
 289:         while idx < len(lines) and lines[idx].strip():
 290:             text_lines.append(lines[idx].strip())
 291:             idx += 1
 292:         text = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", " ".join(text_lines)).strip()
 293:         if text:
 294:             segments.append((start, end, text))
 295:     return segments
 296: 
 297: 
 298: def _parse_timecode(token: str) -> Optional[float]:
 299:     match = _TIMECODE_RE.search(token)
 300:     if match is None:
 301:         return None
 302:     h = int(match.group("h"))
 303:     m = int(match.group("m"))
 304:     s = int(match.group("s"))
 305:     ms = int(match.group("ms"))
 306:     return float(h * 3600 + m * 60 + s) + ms / 1000.0
 307: 
 308: 
 309: def _build_segments_from_text(raw: str, duration_seconds: float) -> List[SrtSegment]:
 310:     rows = [re.sub(r"\s+", " ", item).strip() for item in raw.splitlines() if item.strip()]
 311:     if not rows:
 312:         return []
 313:     duration = max(1.0, float(duration_seconds or 0.0))
 314:     step = max(1.5, duration / max(1, len(rows)))
 315:     cursor = 0.0
 316:     segments: List[SrtSegment] = []
 317:     for row in rows:
 318:         end = min(duration, cursor + step)
 319:         if end <= cursor:
 320:             end = cursor + 1.0
 321:         segments.append((cursor, end, row))
 322:         cursor = end
 323:     return segments

      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIf a decision is necessary and cannot be discovered from local context, ask the user directly.   "user_instructions": "## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: C:/Users/ai/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}
